review_title: Infosistema DMM Review: The Indispensable Specialist with Clear Boundaries
description: The overall experience with Infosistema DMM is overwhelmingly positive, as long as expectations are aligned with its purpose. It is an indispensable tool for any organization seriously invested in OutSystems, delivering a level of efficiency and safety that would be nearly impossible to achieve with manual processes. However, it must be viewed as a specialized component of a company's data toolkit, not the entire toolbox. The experience only sours when the tool is pushed beyond the boundaries of its well-defined niche. For its target audience, it is a solution that delivers on its promise exceptionally well.
author: KLEUBER M.
pros: From a technical presales perspective, the most compelling advantage of Infosistema's DMM is its ability to eliminate the need for manual data migration scripting. In a typical project, this process consumes hundreds of hours of specialized development work, which is both costly and prone to human error.
cons: The most significant drawback is that DMM is exclusively designed for the OutSystems ecosystem. While it excels within that environment, it offers no value for other platforms. For any enterprise with a diverse technology stack (e.g., Salesforce, custom .NET applications, SAP), DMM is a point solution, not a comprehensive data management strategy.
author_duration: Used the software for: 2+ years

review_title: Infosistema DMM - Impression from Low code Developer
author: Nandhakumar S.
pros: Infosistema DMM (Data Migration Manager) has been a game-changer for our data migration, management, and synchronization needs on the OutSystems platform. Its intuitive interface allows for quick setup and efficient migrations, minimizing manual work and letting us focus on development. DMM ensures data consistency across environments, even handling complex data structures seamlessly. The added security and compliance features, including comprehensive logging, have been essential for meeting regulatory standards like GDPR. Its adaptability across various application lifecycle stages makes it an invaluable asset for our team, especially when managing large-scale, multi-environment deployments effectively and securely.
cons: While Infosistema DMM offers impressive features, there are a few areas where it could improve. The initial configuration can be somewhat time-consuming, especially for complex environments, and occasionally, we encountered minor performance issues with larger data sets. The documentation, while helpful, sometimes lacks depth on advanced customization, making certain features less accessible. The tool’s interface, though functional, could also benefit from a more modern, streamlined design to enhance user experience. Despite these points, Infosistema DMM remains a valuable asset for data migration and synchronization, but a few updates could further elevate its usability and performance.
author_duration: Used the software for: 6-12 months

DMM allows us to do so, saving time and money, because developing an application to do these tasks in OutSystems or ETL tools is more expensive than acquiring the DMM product.
cons: Even though exporting the database from OS (source) into external databases works, the logging details are not the best. It is hard to validate that the same number of OutSystems site records matches the ones in the destination database.

review_title: Optimize your data migrations while saving time & money
author: José M.
pros: Data migration processes are typically very complex activities where we tend to use a significant amount of man-hours. With this data migration tool, we were able to migrate data between servers in a more straightforward manner and without all the post-migration hassle of personally ensuring all compliances were followed and all data has been properly migrated. From my perspective the simple manner in which one can easily create a connection to a given DB server/catalogue and create/execute a data migration procedure is the best feature of Infosistema DMM. From a data manager point of view the possibility of performing data scrambling and anonymization to data existing on prod environments before migrating it to dev is also extremely interesting given the need to comply of privacy and security requirements.
cons: From a UI perspective, Infosistema DMM still has a margin for evolving given its current simplicity. Considering it is to be enclosed in an OutSystems environment maybe it could follow existing themes and give users a more “familiar feel” and a more intuitive experience for those using it for the first time.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Finally a tool to perform ETL operations between OutSystems environments without custom development!
description: The ability to easily migrate data between our OutSystems environments without needing to build and maintain our own custom processes. The complexity of our application combined with the rapid rate of change would have otherwise required us to hire an additional developer dedicated primarily to this task. With DMM we have saved thousands of dollars and man hours. Thanks Infosistema!
author: Mick D.
pros: DMM is easy to use and feature rich. It has been a lifesaver for synchronizing our Quality and Test environments using live data from Production. Migration configurations can be saved and loaded on-demand and even scheduled during off-peak hours to minimize impact during peak hours. The ability to filter subsets of data and obfuscate sensitive data such as personally identifiable data is a significant capability for privacy compliance. The responsiveness from support is great and their willingness and ability to implement feature requests in an agile manner is fantastic.
cons: The user interface, while very easy to use, could benefit from some minor improvements when configuring new migrations. With a very large environment containing many modules and entities you may find yourself moving back and forth between two screens until you have located and added all of the tables you want to include, but other than that very minor issue DMM meets our needs perfectly.
